sanskrit:
इहैकस्थं जगत्कृत्स्नं पश्याद्य सचराचरम्।
मम देहे गुडाकेश यच्चान्यद्द्रष्टुमिच्छसि॥
transliteration:
ihaika-sthaṁ jagat kṛtsnaṁ paśyādya sa-carācaram
mama dehe guḍākeśa yac cānyad draṣṭum icchasi
literal_meaning: In this one place—see now—the entire universe, moving and unmoving, O Guḍākeśa, and whatever else you wish to see.
contextual_meaning: Krishna offers Arjuna a single-pointed, panoramic vision of totality—past, present, future, inner and outer—compressed into the here-and-now of the therapist’s office (the chariot). It is an invitation to confront the whole gestalt of one’s life at once, dissolving the normal filters of time, space, and ego.
psychological_state: Peak or pre-peak mystical experience: simultaneous expansion and threat of ego-annihilation; akin to the moment before a psychedelic journey or life-review in near-death experiences.
trigger_scenario: A client who has just received a terminal diagnosis, or who is about to undergo a major life transition (divorce, emigration, retirement), suddenly sees every memory, relationship, and future possibility flash before them in one overwhelming instant.
anchor_text: See your whole life at once—past, present, future—without flinching.
